protocol_name: Broth microdilution antimicrobial susceptibility testing (MIC determination)
paper_source: Clinical and Laboratory Standards Institute. Methods for Dilution Antimicrobial Susceptibility Tests for Bacteria That Grow Aerobically. CLSI standard M07, 12th ed. 2024.

sequential_steps.notes: Prepare a bacterial suspension by picking a test organism from an overnight agar culture into 3 mL sterile saline; exact source and destination vessel locations are not specified.
Adjust turbidity of the saline suspension to between 0.5 and 1 McFarland; no robotic mixing volume or repetitions stated.
Prepare inoculating suspension using 12 mL Mueller-Hinton broth in a tube or vessel; exact locations not specified.
Add 25 uL of the previously prepared bacterial suspension to 12 mL Mueller-Hinton broth to make the inoculating suspension; vessel positions not stated.
Mix the inoculating suspension evenly; exact mixing parameters are not given.
Bring the refrigerated dry antimicrobial microtiter plate to room temperature before inoculation; no transfer volume provided.
Transfer inoculating suspension into a reservoir; source and destination locations and volume not specified.

Inoculate wells of the antimicrobial microtiter plate with 100 uL bacterial suspension using an 8- or 12-channel pipette, starting with wells containing the lowest drug concentration. Individual well locations are not provided.

Cover plates and incubate under culture conditions specified for each bacterial species. Number of stacked plates should not exceed three. Exact incubation time and temperature are not provided in the extracted source.

extraction_notes: The CLSI M07 sources primarily describe a standard/guideline rather than a single fully enumerated robot-ready protocol.
Exact Opentrons labware names were not stated in the source; closest compatible generic labware were selected for a 96-well microtiter plate, reservoir, and 200 uL tips.

The source specifies use of an 8- or 12-channel pipette and a micropipette, but not Opentrons model names; p300_multi_gen2 and p300_single_gen2 were chosen as closest equivalents because 100 uL transfers are required.
Well-by-well antimicrobial plate layout, source reservoir wells, and destination well coordinates were not provided, so source_location and destination_location are null where appropriate.
The overnight agar culture source plate type was not specified.
The broth microdilution source states to adjust the initial suspension to between 0.5 and 1 McFarland, but does not provide automation-specific measurement or mixing parameters.
Incubation temperature and duration for the WOAH broth microdilution sheet are stated only as species-specific culture conditions and therefore are recorded as null.
The specific antimicrobial agents and concentrations tested are not enumerated in the extracted procedural text, so a generic dried antimicrobial microdilution plate reagent is used.
QC strains are mentioned, but no explicit handling volumes or steps for QC inoculation are provided in the extracted text.
